headline: EPD CEO warns markets underestimate Hormuz closure impact
summary: Investing.com -- Enterprise Products Partners LP Chief Executive Officer Jim Teague said Tuesday that investors are underestimating the potential global supply implications from a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz, adding to industry warnings about disruptions from the Iran war.

raw.headline: EPD CEO warns markets underestimate Hormuz closure impact
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Enterprise Products Partners LP Chief Executive Officer Jim Teague said Tuesday that investors are underestimating the potential global supply implications from a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz, adding to industry warnings about disruptions from the Iran war.

headline: Shell CEO says energy shortages may last into next year
summary: Investing.com -- Shell Plc Chief Executive Officer Wael Sawan said oil and liquefied natural gas shortages caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade could continue for months and potentially into next year.

headline: Procter & Gamble's Portfolio Strategy: Strengthening Core Brands?
summary: PG's innovation-led strategy drives growth, with strong product launches, brand gains and supply chain upgrades reinforcing long-term competitive advantage.

headline: World Bank sees 24% surge in energy prices in 2026
summary: Investing.com -- Energy prices are projected to jump 24% in 2026—the highest since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine—if the worst Middle East war disruptions end by May, according to the World Bank.

headline: UAE to exit OPEC and OPEC+ starting May 1
summary: Investing.com -- The United Arab Emirates will exit OPEC and OPEC+ starting May 1, according to state-run WAM news agency.

raw.headline: UAE to exit OPEC and OPEC+ starting May 1

headline: Why are stocks at highs with Hormuz closed and oil elevated?
summary: Investing.com -- Global stock markets have staged a strong recovery since late March despite the ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and elevated oil prices. In a note to clients on Tuesday, Yardeni Research explained why.

headline: JetBlue misses first quarter profit estimates on fuel costs
summary: Investing.com - JetBlue Airways Corp (NASDAQ:JBLU) reported a first quarter loss of -$0.87 per share on Tuesday, missing analyst estimates of -$0.72 by $0.15. Revenue reached $2.24 billion, matching analyst expectations and representing a 4.7% increase from the prior year period.
